title: Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of 2025
short_title: Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill changes what Medicare will pay for when people see chiropractors. Right now, Medicare only covers spine adjustments, but this bill would let Medicare pay for all the treatments that chiropractors are allowed to do in their state. Before chiropractors can provide these new covered services, they must watch and complete a training video online one time. This would give older Americans on Medicare more treatment options when they visit chiropractors.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings and Statement of Purpose
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. In nineteen seventy two, Medicare started covering chiropractic care for people who qualify for Medicare. However, the old law only allows Medicare patients to get one type of service at chiropractic offices. Medicare coverage for chiropractic care has not kept up with private insurance coverage or other federal health programs. Today, research shows that chiropractic services work well and cost less money. Because of this good evidence, private insurance companies now cover more chiropractic services. State licenses for chiropractors have also improved to better serve patients and get better health results. This law would make Medicare chiropractic coverage more similar to what the Department of Veterans Affairs, Department of Defense, Federal Employee Health Benefits Program, and private health insurance provide. The purpose of this law is to expand how Medicare recognizes and covers doctors of chiropractic as physicians under the Medicare program. This includes their current service of manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation, which is a condition where spine bones are out of their normal position. The coverage would include any function or action that the state legally allows the doctor of chiropractic to perform.

sections.title: Section 3 Providing Medicare Coverage for All Physicians Services Furnished by Doctors of Chiropractic Within the Scope of Their License
sections.text: This section changes the Social Security Act to expand what Medicare covers when doctors of chiropractic provide services. The current law only covers chiropractors for manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation, which is when spine bones are out of their normal position. The new law would cover a doctor of chiropractic who has a license from the state where they work. The license must legally allow them to perform the function or action in that state or area. This section also adds limits on payment for services from certain doctors of chiropractic. Medicare will only pay for these services if one of two things is true. First, the services must be provided by a doctor of chiropractic who has completed an educational training webinar created by the Secretary of Health and Human Services or a similar electronic training program. The doctor only needs to complete this training once and be verified as having attended it. Second, Medicare will pay if the services are treatment using manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation.
